comment: Flow_Classify_Persist (§5.2) — CHILD of intake (Run-a-Child-Flow). Apply-to-each attachment: deterministic classify by extension (graph-intake parity), bytes -> Azure Blob (NEVER inline in a Dataverse row), one Evidence row per attachment carrying the storagePath ref. Returns { payloadHash, instructionBytesB64, instructionName, imageCount } to the parent so intake can hand the instruction PDF to parse. [BUILD]; runs against live mail only when [RESERVED-FOR-USER]. INPUT-CONTRACT FIX (task plan §3.3): the trigger now receives the email ATTACHMENTS array (+subject/from) from intake — the old child only got {messageId,caseId} and read triggerOutputs()?['body/attachments'] (empty for an HTTP child) so it created 0 Evidence rows. Every read is now triggerBody()?['...']. SHA DECISION (task plan §9 Q2 / §3.2 option ii): the stock shared_azureblob CreateFile returns blob metadata (Path), NOT a content hash, and no storage-hash Function exists — so per-attachment SHA-256 dedup is DROPPED for the slice; message-level idempotency is the Message-ID probe (intake) + the payloadHash fold (here). cr1bd_sha256 is left unset until a hashing surface is added; size comes from the Outlook attachment's own size field.

manual.comment: Child flow invoked by intake.definition (Run-a-Child-Flow). Receives messageId, caseId, subject, from, and the ATTACHMENTS array (each item carries name, contentType, size, contentBytes(base64)). Children MUST use 'Manually trigger a flow' (Request) per Learn.

Init_attachmentTokens.comment: Per-attachment dedup/order tokens (name|size) folded into payloadHash. Replaces the SHA array (no content hash available from CreateFile — §3.2).

Init_instructionBytesB64.comment: Captures the FIRST instruction attachment's base64 bytes so intake can pass it straight to parse (the parser connector takes document=base64).

Apply_to_each_attachment.comment: repetitions=1 keeps the shared dedup/count/instruction variables race-free (default parallel iterations corrupt a shared array/scalar mutation).

Compose_kind.comment: Deterministic classify -> cr1bd_kind integer: image=100000000, instruction=100000002, email=100000003, other=100000006. Mirrors graph-intake; do not invent.

Upload_bytes_to_storage.comment: Bytes go to Blob; CreateFile_V2 returns BlobMetadata (Path/Name/Id/...). Row carries only the ref (graph-intake invariant: all file data uploaded to storage immediately). No content hash is returned by the stock connector (§3.2). CONNECTOR RECONCILIATION (2026-06-19): operationId is CreateFile_V2 (path /v2/datasets/{dataset}/files) NOT the deprecated CreateFile (datasets/default/files) — the latter has NO 'dataset' parameter, so passing dataset there fails Flow validation with WorkflowOperationParametersExtraParameter 'dataset'. For the ACCESS-KEY connection (cr1bd_evidenceblob -> account cespkevidstdev01) the dataset value MUST be the literal token 'AccountNameFromSettings' (per the Azure Blob connector known-issue: access-key auth requires dataset='AccountNameFromSettings', not the blob endpoint); the CONTAINER is the first segment of folderPath, hence 'evidence/intake/<messageId>' (was dataset='evidence' + 'intake/...').

Create_Evidence_row.comment: One Evidence row per attachment. imageRole default Unknown=100000003 + registrationVisible=false (manual/OCR tagging until M2) -> the image-rules readiness check in status-evaluate stays FALSE until a human/OCR tags overview+damage_closeup (expected per the data model). storagePath only; never inline bytes. cr1bd_sha256 intentionally unset (no hash surface — §3.2). Bound to the Case when caseId is present (intake always supplies it; null-bind tolerated for a bytes-only pre-resolve call).

If_instruction.comment: Capture the FIRST instruction attachment's bytes+name (PDF/DOCX/DOC) for the parser. Only the first is captured (most intakes carry a single instruction).

If_image.comment: Tally images for the readiness imageCount return; otherwise capture the first instruction for parse.

Fold_payloadHash.comment: Deterministic payloadHash seed: lower+trim subject/from, sort per-attachment name|size tokens ascending, join with newline. (Was the SHA fold; with no content hash available the canonical token is name|size — §3.2.) Returned to the parent for the payloadHash dedup probe.

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: partial attachment persistence - continued others, see run history (§5.2: continue others, audit per-attachment)

Handle_attachment_failure.comment: Runs only on Scope Failed/Skipped/TimedOut: one bad attachment audits and continues; does not fail the whole message.

Respond_to_parent.comment: Returns the outputs the parent (intake) needs: the folded payloadHash, the first instruction PDF bytes+name (for parse), and the accepted image count. Response is the child-flow return action (mirrors 'Respond to a Power App or flow'). runAfter both the success Scope and the partial-failure audit so a partial batch still returns what it captured.
